examTip: Bengal-specific events carry extra weight in WB Police SI. Know exact years and persons.

sections.heading: 📅 Critical Dates — Bengal Focus
rows: Sepoy Mutiny / First War of Independence
Partition of Bengal annulled, Capital shifted Delhi

sections.heading: 🦁 Bengali Freedom Fighters — Must Know
points: Khudiram Bose — youngest martyr, hanged 1908, age 18 (Muzaffarpur bomb case)
Surya Sen (Masterda) — led Chittagong Armory Raid 18 April 1930, hanged 1934
Bagha Jatin (Jatindra Nath Mukherjee) — fought British soldiers at Balasore 1915, died in battle
Subhash Chandra Bose — born 23 Jan 1897 Cuttack, formed Azad Hind Fauj (INA) 1943
Rabindranath Tagore — renounced Knighthood after Jallianwala Bagh 1919
Bipin Chandra Pal — 'Lal-Bal-Pal' trio with Tilak and Lajpat Rai, extremist nationalist
Aurobindo Ghose — revolutionary turned spiritual, Alipore bomb case 1908
Matangini Hazra — 'Gandhi Buri', shot by police in Tamluk 1942 while hoisting tricolor

sections.heading: 🔥 Major Movements — Quick Summary
points: Swadeshi Movement (1905): Against Partition of Bengal. Boycott of British goods. Use Indian-made goods.
Non-Cooperation Movement (1920-22): Boycott courts, schools, titles. Ended after Chauri Chaura violence.
Civil Disobedience (1930): Gandhi's 241-mile Dandi March to break Salt Law. Mass arrests.
Quit India Movement (1942): 'Do or Die' — Gandhi. August Kranti. British arrested all leaders same night.
INA Trials (1945-46): Shah Nawaz Khan, P.K. Sehgal, G.S. Dhillon tried at Red Fort. National outrage.

sections.heading: ⚡ Slogans & Their Persons
rows: 'Give me blood, I'll give you freedom'
Lala Lajpat Rai (died after lathi charge)

sections.heading: 📚 Bengal Renaissance — Key Persons
points: Raja Ram Mohan Roy — Brahmo Samaj (1828), fought Sati, first Indian to visit England
Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar — widow remarriage, women's education, Bengali alphabet reform
Derozio (H.L.V.) — Young Bengal Movement, Hindu College
Swami Vivekananda — Ramakrishna Mission (1897), Parliament of World's Religions Chicago 1893
Rabindranath Tagore — Nobel Prize Literature 1913 for Gitanjali, first Asian Nobel laureate
